calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ZEDO, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT:

Defendant Zedo, Inc.'s motion to vacate and set aside the default taken against it on April 21, 2020 and the default judgment entered on February 2, 2021 is denied as untimely. The motion was brought on October 4, 2021 under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) on grounds of mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect. "Application for this relief . . . shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken." (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 473(b) (emphasis added).) "The six-month limit is mandatory; a court has no authority to grant relief under section 473, subdivision (b), unless an application is made within the six-month period." (Arumbula v. Union Carbide Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 333, 340.) The Court has no authority to grant relief from the six-month limit, whether based on mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect or an attorney's affidavit of fault. (Id. at 345; accord, Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 918, 918 ["The six-month time limitation is jurisdictional; the court has no power to grant relief under section 473 once the time has lapsed."].) Even if defendant's motion to set aside the default judgment were timely, setting aside the default judgment would be an "idle act" because the underlying default would remain in effect. (Pulte Homes Corp. v. Williams Mechanical, Inc. (2016) 2 Cal.App.5th 267, 273 [trial court erred in granting relief under section 473(b) to defendant whose motion was filed less than six months after entry of the default judgment, but more than six months after entry of its default].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Off Calendar

Request For Default Prove Up Hearing For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant ZEDO, Inc, a California corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Christopher B. Good, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Ruptly GMBH, a Foreign Business Entity.
Polina Garuseva, CFO and COO for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Mr. Good and Ms. Garuseva appeared via ZOOM for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendant ZEDO, Inc., a California corporation.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Written Contract, Breach of Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing, Account Stated and Open Book Account was filed on February 27, 2020, and personally served on defendant ZEDO, Inc.'s authorized Agent of Process on March 3, 2020.
Default as to defendant ZEDO, Inc, a California corporation, was entered on April 21, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on November 20, 2020.

Plaintiff Ruptly GMBH, a Foreign Business Entity, submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Media Buy Contract between plaintiff & defendant dated 8/20/2014...........ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Reconciliation Statement dated 3/13/2019...................................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Ruptly GMBH, a Foreign Business Entity, and against the defaulted defendant ZEDO, Inc., a California corporation, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $70,052.65.
Prejudgment Interest - $11,878.61.
Costs - $665.00.

Total - $82,596.26.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.
